name: North Atlantic Sprint
rationale: The North Atlantic is the world's busiest and most unforgiving ocean crossing. Nine days of sustained head-seas with regular slamming events will progressively accumulate fatigue in the keel, bilge strake welds, and bow flare. A vessel that can't distribute stress across its hull geometry will crack long before Southampton. Primary kill chain: slamming -> weld fatigue -> crack propagation -> hull breach.
